findings.context: considerations. ### **Content production** **Benefits:** AI helps IDs create content more quickly. [88% of respondents to our 2026 AI in L&D Report](https://www.synthesia.io/reports/ai-in-learning-and-development-report-2026)

findings.context: # AI in Instructional Design: How IDs Are Using AI in 2026 ![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/65e89895c5a4b8d764c0d70e/6980c0cd64e0dd8332556329_1516890038764.jpeg)

location.heading: AI in Instructional Design: How IDs Are Using AI in 2026

findings.sentence: AI in Instructional Design: How IDs Are Using AI in 2026

meta.anchor: over half reported using ChatGPT daily

location.heading: Where AI adoption is strongest today

findings.sentence: In a survey of 70 instructional designers, [over half reported using ChatGPT daily](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11423-024-10437-y), most often for writing learning objectives, brainstorming activities, and drafting content.

meta.anchor: generate aligned objectives, varied activities, and detailed rubrics in one session

location.heading: Where AI adoption is strongest today

findings.sentence: A hands-on study testing ChatGPT as a course-building tool found it could [generate aligned objectives, varied activities, and detailed rubrics in one session](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11528-024-00967-w), but it also fabricated references, missed the online-course format entirely, and produced shallow assessments that wouldn't challenge real learners.

meta.anchor: they used only a small subset of the safeguards the research recommends

location.heading: Where AI adoption is strongest today

findings.sentence: When organizations deployed AI in HR, [they used only a small subset of the safeguards the research recommends](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0160791X24000757).
I've described this pattern as [readiness debt](https://elearningindustry.com/the-growing-readiness-debt-in-ld).
[Research from UC Berkeley Haas](https://hbr.org/2026/02/ai-doesnt-reduce-work-it-intensifies-it) found that AI didn't reduce work, it intensified it.

[A separate survey](https://www.wsj.com/lifestyle/careers/ai-work-free-time-51c8c92a) found workers using AI save nearly an hour a day on average, but the reward for working faster is often just more work.
(This guide can help you sort the [model differences](https://drphilippahardman.substack.com/p/the-ai-tool-stack-for-instructional).)

location.heading: **How to use AI to measure whether learning is working**

findings.sentence: ‍ [Transfer research](https://eric.ed.gov/?id=EJ371658&) has long shown that outcomes depend on what happens after training in the work environment.
